title: Legal Counseling - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Legal Counseling 8 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Disabilities Issues Ex-Offenders Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF DISABILITY RIGHTS OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy and limited legal services to people with disabilities. DRO's individual advocacy focuses on providing callers with information and materials about their rights to help them advocate for themselves. Occasionally, Disability Rights Oregon will engage in litigation to protect the rights of Oregonians under applicable federal and state laws. DRO does not provide the following services: -Representation in criminal or family law matters -Help finding employment, housing, or financial assistance -Case management -Assistance for short term disability issues -Transportation to doctor's office -Applying for or appealing denial of Social Security disability benefits -Estates and trusts -Becoming the guardian of a person with a disability -Out-of-state issues -Workers' compensation -Medical malpractice or personal injury -Consumer or bankruptcy issues -Anything that is not the wish of the person with the disability 900 SW 5th Avenue Suite 1800 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Individuals with disabilities who have legal issues related to their disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-11am and 1pm-4pm Email (503) 243-2081 Get MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY COMMUNITY ACTION REENTRY SERVICES EX-OFFENDER RESOURCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A resource center supporting individuals transitioning from incarceration, those on probation or parole, and others affected by the criminal justice system. Offers weekly legal clinics for civil matters, help with health insurance applications, and classes on parenting, personal finance, tenant readiness, and motivation. Provides consultations, information, and referrals for people and families impacted by incarceration. A computer lab is available for tutoring, GED preparation, job searches, and other needs. 2085 Commercial Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Current or past involvement with criminal justice system. Preference given to those currently on parole supervision and recently released from incarceration. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 332-5332 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST SEASONAL WORKERS ASSOCIATION 11 POINT BENEFIT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency food and clothing, preventive medical care, limited non-emergency dental, legal advice, job referrals, advocacy, and information and referral. Services are prioritized for members. Volunteers are provided with organizational skills and advocacy methods. 203 N Oakdale Avenue Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Seasonal and other low-income workers. Membership required. Services for members and their families are benefits of membership. Hours: 7 days per week 9am-9pm, including holidays Email (541) 773-6811 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON CONSUMER JUSTICE CONSUMER RIGHTS RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides tools and information to assist in exercising consumer rights. Resources include handouts, articles, videos, research, and comic books to support consumers in taking action against unlawful practices and holding companies accountable. Also provides connection to legal experts (e.g., direct representation, advice) for those facing consumer-related law issues. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 406-3311 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUTH, RIGHTS & JUSTICE FAMILY DEFENSE PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works with parents whose children are at risk of entering the foster care system. Addresses legal and resource needs such as child custody, legal counseling, and restraining orders. Focus is to empower families, protect children, and prevent foster care placement. 1785 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 300 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Households that have an open ODHS case with risk of child removal Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 803-8640 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DISABILITY RIGHTS OREGON DISABILITY RIGHTS OREGON LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION FAMILY EMPOWERMENT LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION FAMILY EMPOWERMENT LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION FAMILY EMPOWERMENT MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY EX-OFFENDER RESOURCE PROGRAM NORTHWEST SEASONAL WORKERS ASSOCIATION 11 POINT BENEFIT PROGRAM YOUTH, RIGHTS & JUSTICE FAMILY DEFENSE PROJECT
